entities.name: Orchestration engineering
entities.context: Proposed technical strategy for coordinating many small models to achieve high-quality outputs

entities.name: Prompt engineering
entities.context: Contrasted approach focused on crafting prompts for single high-end models

entities.name: Modularity
entities.context: Principle of breaking systems into discrete, independent components to reduce failure risk

entities.name: Error correction
entities.context: Principle of creating inspection points where errors can be caught or diluted

entities.name: Task decomposition
entities.context: Technique of breaking complex tasks into small, predictable steps with multiple checkpoints

entities.name: System resilience
entities.context: Quality that emerges from decomposition rather than relying on single high-capability components

entities.name: Single points of failure
entities.context: Risk pattern where one component (like a hallucinating high-end model) causes catastrophic failure

relationships.context: Contrasted approaches - V believes strategy should shift from prompt engineering to orchestration engineering
Orchestration engineering relies on decomposing tasks into discrete, narrow steps
Task decomposition is an application of the modularity principle
Breaking into steps creates inspection points enabling error correction
System resilience emerges from modular design rather than single-shot approaches
High-end model prompts create single points of failure risk like hallucination
